trustBar.signal3: No cost if we don't collect

home.heroH1Line2: Protect your cash flow and reputation.
home.heroSubtitle: We recover unpaid B2B invoices up to $15K. You only pay when we collect.
home.problem1Title: Your cash flow takes the hit
home.problem1Body: You finished a $5,000 subcontracting job. The contractor promises to pay, then goes silent. You need that $5,000 for materials, payroll, and your next project.
home.problem2Body: You've spent months chasing a client over an unpaid invoice. Every ignored follow-up takes time away from serving clients who actually pay on time.
home.step1Description: We schedule a brief consultation to evaluate your situation and map out the best approach.
home.step2Description: Send us your invoices, contracts, and any evidence related to the debt via email, WhatsApp, or text message.
home.step3Description: Webster handles the rest. We run every offer by you and ask before escalating.
home.processSubtitle: We only escalate when necessary, and only with your approval.
home.stage1Description: We assess your situation and decide together on the best approach, from a friendly reminder to a formal demand letter. We negotiate directly with the debtor and keep you informed. Most cases are resolved here.
home.mockupDebtorMessage: Hello, we received your communication. We will not pay. The products were defective.
home.mockupWebsterMessage: Thank you for responding to our email. We reviewed the documentation for this transaction. Attached is a signed receipt from your staff confirming the products were received in good condition. Do you have any evidence you can send us showing the products arrived defective?
home.stage2Description: If negotiation is not enough, we file a collection lawsuit to obtain a judgment in your favor.
home.stage3Description: If necessary, we pursue garnishment or other legal mechanisms to enforce the judgment.
home.why1Body: Give your business breathing room by collecting the money your clients haven't paid.
home.why2Body: Stop chasing clients to get paid.
home.why3Body: Show your clients that your business has serious backing.
home.why4Body: A firm but respectful attorney who makes it easy for them to pay. Your clients stay happy. Your reputation stays intact.
home.aiPullQuote: The strength of a firm. The speed of technology.
home.aiPoint1: We use AI to draft legal documents, analyze your files, and coordinate every stage of the collection process.
home.aiPoint2: AI ensures consistency and precision at every step, eliminating the errors that make the traditional process expensive and slow.
home.aiPoint3: Every document is reviewed and signed by an attorney before it's sent.
home.pricingSubtitle: Simple fees. No surprises. We help with unpaid invoices from $1,000 to $15,000.
home.pricingDisclaimer: Zero deposits or hourly billing. You only pay when we collect.
home.faq1A: No. Webster is a law firm, not a collection agency. We prioritize preserving your business relationship before taking any serious legal action.
home.faq2Q: What's the minimum amount to work with Webster?
home.faq2A: Webster handles unpaid invoices from $1,000 to $15,000. If your case falls outside this range, we'll let you know during the free consultation and point you in the right direction.
home.faq3Q: How does the fee model work?
home.faq3A: Webster works on contingency. We only charge a percentage of what we recover for you. If we don't recover anything, you owe nothing.
home.faq4Q: How long does the process take?
home.faq4A: It depends on the complexity and the debtor's response. Many cases are resolved within two to four weeks before any legal action. Cases that go to court can take several months.
home.faq5Q: What documents do I need to get started?
home.faq5A: Generally we need: the original invoice or contract, proof of the debt (emails, purchase orders, delivery receipts), and any prior communication with the debtor. During the initial consultation, we'll tell you exactly what applies to your case.
home.faq6Q: Can I submit more than one invoice?
home.faq6A: Yes. Many businesses have several unpaid invoices. We evaluate each one and give you a complete strategy.
home.faq7Q: Will the debtor know I hired Webster?
home.faq7A: Yes. We communicate directly with the debtor on your behalf. All communication is professional, clear, and backed by an attorney.
home.faq8Q: Does Webster handle cases outside Puerto Rico?
home.faq8A: We currently focus on debts under Puerto Rico law. If your case involves another jurisdiction, we can evaluate it during the free consultation.
home.faq9Q: What if the debtor can't pay?
home.faq9A: During the initial consultation, we assess whether collection is realistic. If we determine the debtor can't pay or recovery is unlikely, we tell you upfront. We don't take cases without a reasonable chance of success.

blog.subtitle: Legal updates, collection strategies, and business law news from Puerto Rico.
blog.featuredTitle: Five mistakes businesses make when collecting overdue invoices
blog.featuredExcerpt: Many businesses wait too long to act when a client doesn't pay. In this article, we explain the most common mistakes and how to avoid them to protect your cash flow.
blog.sidebarNewsletterSubtitle: Get articles and legal updates straight to your inbox.
blog.post1Title: How to protect your payments before it's too late
blog.post1Excerpt: Prevention is the best collection strategy. Learn how to structure your contracts and billing to minimize the risk of non-payment.
blog.post2Title: What to do when a client doesn't pay: A practical guide
blog.post2Excerpt: A step-by-step guide on the legal options available when a business client fails to pay an invoice.
blog.post3Title: Before court vs. in court: When to escalate collection
blog.post3Excerpt: Not every case requires a lawsuit. We explain how to evaluate when out-of-court action is enough and when it's time to litigate.
blog.post4Title: The real cost of unpaid invoices on your business
blog.post4Excerpt: Overdue invoices affect more than your balance sheet. They affect your ability to operate, invest, and grow.
blog.post5Title: Contract clauses that protect your right to get paid
blog.post5Excerpt: The right clauses in your contracts can be the difference between getting paid and eating the loss. These are the essentials.
blog.post6Title: Debt collection in Puerto Rico: What every business owner should know
blog.post6Excerpt: An introduction to the legal framework for business debt collection in Puerto Rico, including time limits, jurisdiction, and available remedies.

contacto.label: Webster · Puerto Rico
contacto.subtitle: Owed money by a client? Schedule a free consultation with an attorney.
contacto.infoSubtitle: Write to us or call us. We'll get back to you as soon as possible.
contacto.ctaSubtitle: Fill out the form and an attorney will contact you.
contacto.disclaimer: By completing the form, you agree that a Webster attorney will contact you to evaluate your case.

contadores.label: For Accountants · Webster Legal
contadores.h1: Become the accountant who solves the client's problems.
contadores.subtitle: When a client's receivables haven't moved in months, refer them to us. Webster recovers unpaid B2B invoices with real legal backing.
contadores.sectionTitle: A collection agency is not the same as a law firm.
contadores.bullet1Bold: An attorney letter changes the conversation.
contadores.bullet1Rest: Debtors cooperate with attorneys because they want to avoid bigger legal problems.
contadores.bullet2Rest: Webster looks after your client's interests with higher ethical duties than a collection agency.
contadores.bullet3Bold: Webster has multiple tools at its disposal.
contadores.bullet3Rest: Webster can use the court system if the debtor refuses to pay.
contadores.howTitle: Simple for you. Simple for your client.
contadores.step1Description: Tell them to call or fill out the form.
contadores.step2Description: Clear answer: what we can do, what the strategy is, when to expect results.
contadores.step3Description: Most cases are resolved before reaching court, with both parties satisfied.
contadores.ctaFinalText: Next time a client mentions or you identify an unpaid invoice, you know who to recommend.

sobreNosotros.intro: Webster is a law firm that uses AI to deliver faster, more precise legal services. We automate the processes that usually make legal work expensive so our clients get high-quality service at lower cost.
sobreNosotros.founderBio: Graduated from the U.P.R. School of Law with high honors. Adrián Jiménez is a licensed attorney in Puerto Rico with six years of experience in federal and state commercial litigation. He served as a law clerk at the U.S. District Court for the District of Puerto Rico under Judges Gustavo Gelpí and Silvia Carreño Coll. He also worked at the law firms DLA Piper (PR) and Pietrantoni Méndez & Álvarez.
sobreNosotros.ctaSubtitle: Schedule a free consultation with an attorney.
